_comment: Local-log TOKEN-COST provider registry for ai-spend-audit. Each entry points at on-disk session files a tool writes WITH per-message token usage (or pre-computed USD). Built-in formats (builtin:claude/codex/xdt) have dedicated readers and always run when their data exists; their entries here are documentation. `format` selects the reader: generic_jsonl | pi_jsonl | opencode_sqlite | builtin:*. Adding a tool that writes simple per-message token JSONL = one generic_jsonl entry, no code. Absent roots no-op silently (tool not installed), so enabled adapters are safe to ship. SCOPE: this skill ONLY covers tools that write reliable local per-token data. Tools that expose usage solely via an authenticated quota API/cookie (Gemini CLI, Amp, Grok, DeepSeek, Cursor, Copilot, Windsurf) are intentionally NOT supported — their numbers are %/dollars not tokens, the auth is fragile, and (e.g. Cursor) tokens aren't even exposed. Format knowledge below was verified against steipete/CodexBar.
_field_map_keys: For generic_jsonl `fields`: type_key/type_value (optional row filter), model, ts (ISO/epoch s/epoch ms; omit to window by file mtime), input, output, cache_read, cache_write — each is a dotted path OR a list of dotted-path aliases (first present wins). cached_in_input:true subtracts cache_read from input when the provider's input count already INCLUDES cache (e.g. Gemini promptTokenCount). Per-entry `billing` (metered|subscription) overrides model-derived billing for the whole tool.

providers.name: Claude Code
providers._note: read_claude(): $CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR/projects, ~/.config/claude/projects, ~/.claude/projects. Streaming dedup by (message.id,requestId) + 5m/1h cache split + fast-mode (usage.speed). Vertex-routed Claude lands in the same logs (model carries @date) and is priced by family.

providers.name: Codex
providers._note: read_codex(): $CODEX_HOME, ~/.codex, XDT codex-home. Per-turn priority via ~/.codex/logs_2.sqlite (service_tier) + >272k threshold tier. Host=rollout originator (XDT only when orca worker/lead).

providers.name: XDT Maker
providers._note: read_xdt_*(): auto-detected only if the XDT sqlite DB exists. Provides dedup metadata + daily_spend real-cash ledger + schedules + agent_meta fallback.

providers.name: Pi
providers._note: read_pi(): faithful port of CodexBar PiSessionCostScanner. A `model_change` line sets the current (provider, model); each assistant `message` carries message.usage (many aliases). Pi proxies upstreams: openai-codex→gpt-5.x pricing (cache-write folded into input), anthropic→claude pricing (cache-write separate). Rows with an unknown provider tag are dropped. Pi's own plan = subscription. Enabled (no-ops if ~/.pi absent); verify field shape against real logs before trusting $ on a machine that has Pi.

providers.name: OpenCode
providers._note: read_opencode_sqlite(): OpenCode-Go stores a PRE-COMPUTED USD cost per assistant message in opencode.db (json_extract data.$.cost, role=assistant, providerID=opencode-go). We book the dollars directly; there is NO token breakdown so these sessions don't appear in token charts. Real spend → metered. The non-Go OpenCode is cookie/web quota-only → out of scope (see _not_supported).

providers.name: OpenClaw
providers._note: read_openclaw(): OpenClaw is a multi-agent framework. Each agent nests a STANDARD Codex home at agents/<agent>/agent/codex-home (read with the Codex parser incl. per-home priority via that home's logs_2.sqlite) — authoritative for codex-backed turns. Native session logs agents/<agent>/sessions/*.jsonl (trajectory `model.completed` events + newer `message` logs) are read ONLY for non-Codex backends (anthropic/bedrock/…); codex rows there are skipped to avoid double-counting #1. All attributed to source 'OpenClaw'. billing:'' means derive per model family (codex→subscription, claude→metered). No-op if ~/.openclaw absent. Run the engine ON the machine that has OpenClaw (e.g. via ssh) since it reads local files.

providers.name: _example-generic-tool
providers._note: TEMPLATE for ANY tool that writes per-message token usage to JSONL. Copy it, set name/roots/fields/billing, flip enabled:true. Each field accepts a dotted path or an alias list (first match wins). Verify against the tool's real logs, then set format_verified:true.

_not_supported: Intentionally OUT OF SCOPE — tools without reliable local per-token logs: Gemini CLI (OAuth quota only), Amp (ampcode.com cookie + HTML), Grok (grok.com RPC/cookie; local signals.json has unpriced counts only), DeepSeek (API balance/usage endpoint), Cursor & Windsurf (Cursor stores tokens only for chat-mode bubbles — agent mode is 0 — and exposes NO tokens server-side, only dollar/percent quota behind a fragile browser cookie; CodexBar itself sets supportsTokenCost:false for Cursor). These were evaluated and dropped on purpose: their output is %/dollars not tokens, and the auth is brittle. Adding guessed field maps for them would silently produce fake numbers.
_roadmap_local_token_tools: Candidates that DO write local per-token (or per-cost) data — paths from general knowledge, NOT yet verified, so add as enabled:false until confirmed on a machine that has them: (1) Continue.dev — `~/.continue/dev_data/**/*.jsonl` token events (generic_jsonl). (2) Aider — `~/.aider/analytics.jsonl` / `.aider.chat.history` events with tokens+cost. (3) Cline / Roo Code (VS Code extensions) — globalStorage `.../saoudrizwan.claude-dev/tasks/<id>/ui_messages.json` carries per-task tokensIn/Out + apiCost (JSON; would need a small dedicated reader like opencode_sqlite). (4) Zed assistant — conversations under `~/.config/zed` / Application Support. Verify the real shape first, then add + set format_verified:true.
